findings.title: Daemon instantiated GitHubQueue directly, bypassing the throttle plugin
findings.detail: daemon.ts used `new Octokit({ auth: token })` and `new GitHubQueue(octokit, ...)` directly instead of the `createGitHubQueue` factory. The factory wires @octokit/plugin-throttling which handles GitHub rate-limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining / Retry-After). Without it, bursts of API calls hit 429s and crash the daemon. The `octokit` variable used by makeProductionFactory still needs its own Octokit instance — this is separate from the queue's Octokit.
findings.fix_sketch: Import `createGitHubQueue` from `../queue/github.js` and replace `new GitHubQueue(new Octokit(...), ...)` with `await createGitHubQueue({ repos: ..., token: githubToken })`. Keep a separate `new Octokit({ auth: githubToken })` instance for the production factory (makeProductionFactory). Re-add `import type { GitHubQueue }` for the legacyQueueShim signature.

findings.title: Sprint hangs forever if store.saveTask throws after running.set()
findings.detail: In SprintManager.dispatch(), `this.running.set(task.id, ...)` was called before `store.saveTask('running', ...)`. If saveTask threw (e.g. SQLite locked), the catch block called `registry.release(workerId)` but never `this.running.delete(task.id)`. The task ID stayed in `this.running` forever — the next tick skipped it as already in-flight, causing a permanent silent hang with no retry.
findings.fix_sketch: Add `this.running.delete(task.id)` as the first statement in the catch block of dispatch(), before `registry.release(workerId)`, so a saveTask failure leaves no stale entry in the running map.

findings.title: Public control-plane returns 202 OK for daemon-only routes (approve, verify, force_pr)
findings.detail: server.ts `onApprove`, `onVerify`, and `onForcePr` only called `console.error(...)` and returned void. The dispatch() call in control-plane.ts then sent `{ statusCode: 202, ok: true }`. An operator routing `/approve`, `[Verify retry]`, or `[Force-PR]` to port 3001 (public) instead of 3002 (daemon) received a success response but the task was never unblocked. The stale comment claimed 'do not throw — a throw after res.json(202) produces an unhandled exception' but dispatch is awaited before the 202 is sent, so throwing is safe.
findings.fix_sketch: Change onApprove/onVerify/onForcePr to throw `new ControlPlaneClientError(...)`. Update the dispatch() catch block in control-plane.ts to detect ControlPlaneClientError and return 422 (same pattern as sprint_goal path). This ensures callers get a proper error response directing them to port 3002.

findings.title: recoverStale NaN bug: Math.max(1, NaN) = NaN silently skips all recovery
findings.detail: store.ts recoverStale() parsed IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS with `Math.max(1, Number(env ?? 5))`. If the env var was set to a non-numeric string, Number() returns NaN and Math.max(1, NaN) = NaN. SQLite treats NaN as NULL when bound to a parameterized query; NULL comparisons (`attempts >= NULL`, `attempts < NULL`) are UNKNOWN in SQL — no rows match. All stale in_flight tasks stayed permanently stuck and no recovery occurred, with zero log output indicating failure.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace with: `const envAttempts = Number(env ?? ''); const maxAttempts = Number.isFinite(envAttempts) && envAttempts >= 1 ? Math.floor(envAttempts) : 5;` This falls back to 5 for missing or non-numeric values without ever producing NaN.

findings.title: requireEnv rejects legitimate env values '0' and 'false' as missing
findings.detail: utils/env.ts requireEnv() used `if (!v)` which is falsy — it throws for `undefined`, `""`, `"0"`, and `"false"`. A future env var legitimately set to `"0"` or `"false"` (e.g. IFLEET_PLAN_ONLY=false) would cause a startup crash with a misleading 'missing required env var' error instead of using the provided value.
findings.fix_sketch: Change to `if (v === undefined || v === '')` to correctly reject only truly absent or empty-string values while accepting '0', 'false', and other valid string values.

findings.title: server.ts SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers swallow shutdown() rejection, leaving process hung
findings.detail: server.ts used `void shutdown().then(() => process.exit(0))`. If shutdown() rejected (e.g. cp.stop() threw), the `.then` callback never ran, process.exit was never called, and the process hung indefinitely — PM2 would wait for it forever, blocking restarts. The rejection was silently swallowed because the void-cast promise had no .catch().
findings.fix_sketch: Add a `.catch` handler that logs the error and calls `process.exit(1)` so shutdown failures always terminate the process with a non-zero exit code.

findings.title: daemon.ts uses bare Number() on port/tick env vars without NaN guard
findings.detail: daemon.ts parsed `CONTROL_PLANE_PORT` and `IFLEET_DAEMON_TICK_MS` with bare `Number(...)`. If either env var was set to a non-numeric value, Number() returns NaN. The daemon would then listen on port NaN (Node.js coerces this to port 0, picking a random ephemeral port — not 3002) and tick every NaN ms (setTimeout treats NaN as 0, triggering a hot-loop). Neither failure produced an error log.
findings.fix_sketch: Add `Number.isFinite()` guards with fallback to the DEFAULT constant for both port and tick interval.

findings.title: idempotencyKey has no length cap in parseCommand
findings.detail: control-plane.ts parseCommand accepts any length for `idempotencyKey`. It is stored in the SQLite tasks table in the `idempotency_key` column. An attacker (or buggy client) can send a multi-MB key, wasting DB storage and causing the store insert to be slow. Unlike `goal` which has a 10KB cap, idempotencyKey had no limit.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a MAX_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_LEN = 512 guard and throw if exceeded, consistent with the goal cap pattern.

findings.title: attachLineReader in spawn-runner.ts has unbounded line buffer
findings.detail: spawn-runner.ts attachLineReader() buffered subprocess stdout/stderr in a string `buf` and only flushed on newlines. A subprocess that emits gigabytes without a newline (e.g. a runaway binary writing a huge blob) would grow `buf` without bound until the daemon OOM-crashed. The buffer had no cap.
findings.fix_sketch: After processing all newline-terminated lines, check if `buf.length > MAX_LINE_BUF_BYTES` (1 MiB) and emit+drain in chunks to prevent unbounded growth. The partial chunk is emitted as a synthetic line.

findings.title: Dead `void cfg` suppressor in maybeEmbedSprint (scorer.ts)
findings.detail: scorer.ts maybeEmbedSprint had `void cfg;` inside the function body to suppress a TypeScript unused-variable warning on the `cfg: ProposerConfig` parameter. The comment said 'cfg.embeddingModel is reserved for future per-call overrides.' Using `_cfg` as the parameter name is the conventional TypeScript pattern for intentionally unused parameters.
findings.fix_sketch: Rename parameter from `cfg` to `_cfg` in the function signature and remove the `void cfg;` statement.
